speaker: DOCTOR
text: Good morning, Mr. Smith. What brings you in?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I've been really tired for months now. And I think I'm losing weight without trying.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: How much weight, and over what period?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Maybe fifteen pounds over the last three months. My clothes are loose.

speaker: PATIENT
text: Actually, my appetite is okay — sometimes I feel hungrier than usual, but the weight keeps coming off.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I wake up damp sometimes, yeah. Not every night.

speaker: PATIENT
text: Sleep is okay. Mood, I guess I've been down, but I think it's because I feel so tired all the time.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You said fifteen pounds — are you sure about that number?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Let me think. It might be closer to twenty. I was 185, and I weighed myself this morning at 165.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any bowel changes, blood in stool, or urinary changes?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I go to the bathroom more than I used to. I'm thirsty all the time.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any lumps, or a history of cancer in the family?

speaker: PATIENT
text: No lumps I've noticed. My mother had breast cancer at sixty.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I take metformin for pre-diabetes, and levothyroxine for my thyroid.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I'd like to get some labs — a CBC, TSH, metabolic panel, and hemoglobin A1c.
